converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Mary Hitchcock Wilde papers
Wilde, Mary Hitchcock, 1905-
3989
6.7 cubic feet. 6.7 cubic feet.
1801-1984. Papers, clippings, publications, writings, correspondence, photographs. Letters, photographs and photograph albums, writings and genealogical material of Hitchcock-Wilde and related families including Fairbrothers, Frost, Mann, Marvin, Raymond, Reynolds, Stenson, and Waddy. Collection material in English 2082639
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M.E. Warren
Date completed:
August 1986
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, July 2003
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, November 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Letters, photographs and photograph albums, writings and genealogical material of Hitchcock-Wilde and related families including Fairbrothers, Frost, Mann, Marvin, Raymond, Reynolds, Stenson, and Waddy. Letters, writings, and papers concern the military career of Captain Stephen Henry Hitchcock (1808-1857) who aided in the Texas war for independence from Mexico and to the land he acquired in Texas. Reynolds family papers (1837-1893) are mainly letters of Mary Ann Hitchcock and her son Lieutenant Edward McDonald. Wilde family papers are from William Raymond Wilde (1861-1926), and Mary Louise Stenson Wilde (1883-1959), parents of Mary Hitchcock Wilde.
Papers of Mary Hitchcock Wilde include writings as a child; student writings published in The Budget, school magazine of The Vail-Deane School in Elizabeth, N.J.; other published and unpublished writings; personal correspondence; financial records; and correspondence with Arthur Johnson Eames, and other botanists in regard to research on gymnosperms; also, correspondence and records pertaining to the Eames Memorial Fund; and photographs of Wilde's furnishings used as an inventory after her death. In addition, there are diplomas, certificates, indentures, calling cards, greeting cards, auction catalogs, and other memorabilia.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Papers of Hitchcock, Reynolds, Wilde Families Boxes 1-4
Series II. Mary Hitchcock Wilde Papers Boxes 5-7
Series III. Hitchcock-Wilde Family Photographs and Other Items Boxes 8-20, mapcase
Cite As:
Mary Hitchcock Wilde papers, #3989. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Botanist. Mary Hitchcock Wilde, 1905-1984, graduated from the Vail-Dean School in Elizabeth, NJ, where she was enrolled in a college preparatory course. During that time many of her writings were published in the schools publication, The Budget. She studied at the New Jersey College for Women (now Douglas College) and received a Ph.D. in botany from Cornell University in 1942.
Photographs. Women scientists. Botanists. Children\'s writings. Botany. Gymnosperms. Texas -- History -- Revolution, 1835-1836. Families -- New Jersey -- Bayonne. Eames, Arthur J., 1881-1969. Hitchcock, Stephen Henry. Hitchcock, Mary Ann. McDonald, Edward. Wilde, William Raymond. Wilde, Mary Louise Stenson. Hitchcock family. Wilde family. Fairbrothers family. Frost family. Mann family. Marvin family. Raymond family. Reynolds family. Stenson family. Waddy family. New Jersey College for Women Cornell University Vail-Deane School (Elizabeth, NJ)
